_comment: Synthetic health insurance claims only (motor dropped per 2026-07-17 scope decision). No real claimants, policies, or insurers. 'expected_bucket' and 'expected_signals' are dev-only ground truth for validating the Day 3 signal engine — they are NOT rendered onto the PDF.

claims.narrative: The insured was admitted to City Care Hospital on 01-Jun-2026 with acute abdominal pain and was diagnosed with acute appendicitis. An emergency laparoscopic appendectomy was performed the same day. The insured was discharged after a 3-day stay with no complications. Discharge summary, itemized hospital bill, and pre-authorization approval are attached.

The insured underwent a routine day-care cataract surgery (phacoemulsification, left eye) at a Sanjeevani network eye-care center on 10-May-2026. Pre-authorization was obtained in advance. The insured has a documented history of annual ophthalmology check-ups under this policy since 2021. No complications were reported and the insured was discharged the same day.

claims.diagnosis: Fever and body ache, initially described as viral infection
claims.narrative: The insured was admitted on 01-Jul-2026 reporting symptoms initially described as 'fever and body ache consistent with a viral infection.' However, the attached hospital bill and procedure code reflect a coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) surgery performed the same week. The discharge summary does not clearly explain the diagnostic path from the initial viral-infection complaint to open-heart surgery, and no cardiology consultation notes from prior to admission have been provided.

claims.diagnosis: Hip and lower back injury from a fall
claims.narrative: The insured was hospitalized on 25-Jun-2026 following a fall, reportedly sustaining injuries to the hip and lower back. The claimant's statement describes the fall as having occurred 'somewhere near home, possibly on the stairs or the driveway,' without further detail on witnesses, time, or exact location. This is the claimant's third hospitalization claim in the past 12 months under this policy; the itemized bill for the current admission (₹1,80,000) is notably higher than the claimant's two prior hospitalization claims combined.

The insured was admitted to City Care Hospital on 05-Jun-2026 for a scheduled laparoscopic cholecystectomy (gallbladder removal) following a diagnosis of symptomatic gallstones confirmed by ultrasound over the preceding month. Pre-authorization was obtained two weeks in advance. The itemized bill reflects standard costs for the procedure plus a 2-day stay, though total charges are somewhat higher than typical due to the hospital's premium room category selected by the insured.

The insured was admitted to City Care Hospital on 15-May-2026 for a full-term delivery. Labor progressed without complications and the insured delivered vaginally, followed by a 2-day observation stay for mother and newborn. Antenatal records consistent with the policy's maternity waiting period, discharge summary, and itemized bill are attached.

The insured underwent an elective laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair at Sanjeevani Multispecialty Hospital on 18-Apr-2026, following a diagnosis confirmed on clinical examination the prior month. Pre-authorization was approved in advance. The insured was discharged after a 2-day stay with no complications; discharge summary and itemized bill are attached.

claims.diagnosis: Meniscus tear (left knee), confirmed by MRI
claims.narrative: The insured underwent a day-care arthroscopic procedure on the left knee on 05-Jul-2026 to address a meniscus tear identified on MRI. Pre-authorization was submitted and approved. The procedure and 1-day observation were completed without complications; discharge summary and itemized bill are attached.

claims.diagnosis: Fractured left wrist from a workplace fall
claims.narrative: The insured slipped on a wet floor at their workplace cafeteria on 30-May-2026, sustaining a fractured left wrist. A colleague witnessed the fall and assisted the insured to the hospital the same day. Closed reduction was performed and a cast applied; the insured was discharged after a 1-day stay. Incident report from the employer and hospital records are attached.
